content.claim: Poland's 2017 reform reverting to 8+4 structure from 6+3+3 drew criticism primarily for speed of implementation and transition mechanics: approximately 7,000 junior high schools were closed, approximately 10,000 teachers faced potential job loss, and in double-cohort years Warsaw alone saw 3,173 students unable to secure secondary school placement.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Illustrates implementation costs but does not isolate whether these costs are specific to the reversal direction or inherent to any large structural reform
Does not control for whether comparable implementation costs would have occurred under alternative reform pathways
Polish political context (PiS majority, limited consultation) may not be representative of reform feasibility in other governance structures
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
